description: Minimal Claude-Code-like Python coding agent. Features track both product work (test framework, harness scaffold) and product-roadmap work (init/audit commands, memory, skills, multi-agent, observability).

state_machine.rule: Only ./init.sh (or a direct call to a feature's 'verification' command with passing output) may set status='done'. Agents may freely move between the other three states. 'done' is sticky — to re-open a done feature, change status back to 'in-progress' AND remove the 'evidence' field.

features.name: Critical fix: stream_iter 真正流式 (不是 batch) + chat log 增量滚动
features.description: 用户报告 TUI 无流式输出、无法滚动。Root cause: stream_iter 用 yield from asyncio.run(_collect()) 把所有事件收集到一个 list 才 yield,等于 batch 模式。修复:producer thread + queue.Queue 模式,daemon thread 跑 asyncio.run(_consume()),_consume 把每个 event 立即 put 进 queue,generator body yield from queue。cancel() 加 threading.Event 信号让 producer 提前退出。state machine (content_block_start/delta/stop + tool_use) 和 malformed JSON fallback 完整保留。AsyncAnthropic.messages.stream 的事件顺序 + asyncio.run 作为 test seam 的契约兼容 (返回 list 模式仍能驱动 consumer)。

features.name: TUI Header: subagent row click → dismiss overlay + scroll ChatLog to tool call marker
features.description: Wire the subagent overlay's rows to scroll the ChatLog to the corresponding tool call marker, per spec §4.3.2. Two-part change: (1) REFACTOR — move on_subagent_start/on_subagent_end firing from loom/agent/tools.py::run_task into loom/agent/loop.py::_run_tool_block, where block.id (tool_use_id) is naturally available. The current run_task generates a fresh UUID, but the TUI's ChatLog already keys ToolCallMarker by tool_use_id (`_tool_markers[tool_use_id]`) — using two different IDs forced a brittle mapping table. Using block.id directly eliminates the mapping and makes click → scroll a direct lookup. run_task is now a thin wrapper around spawn_subagent (no callback logic). (2) UI — add SubagentRow(Static, can_focus=True) widget in loom/tui/header.py that posts Header.SubagentRowClicked(tool_use_id) on click; update HeaderOverlay._compose_subagent to yield SubagentRow widgets (was: plain Static). (3) Wire — App.on_subagent_row_clicked handler dismisses the HeaderOverlay (per spec) and scrolls ChatLog to the ToolCallMarker with that tool_use_id (via chat_log._tool_markers[tool_use_id].scroll_visible(top=True, animate=False, immediate=True) + post Update/UpdateScroll messages for guaranteed repaint, matching the pattern in _forward_scroll_to_chatlog). The existing subagent_row spec row format (`<glyph> <id> · <state> · <elapsed>`) is preserved. Tests: new cases in tests/test_tui_header.py for SubagentRow click message posting, app handler dismissing overlay + scrolling, run_task no longer fires callback, _run_tool_block fires for task tools only, callback receives block.id. Eval: new cases in loom/eval/cases/tui_header.py for SubagentRow class defined, Header.SubagentRowClicked message defined, App.on_subagent_row_clicked handler defined, _run_tool_block fires subagent callbacks for task tools. Per spec §4.3.2: 'Clicking a subagent ID inside the Subagent overlay dismisses the overlay and scrolls the ChatLog to that subagent's existing marker. (Markers exist because subagent tool calls are already inline in the chat.)'

features.name: TUI StatusBar: drop the 'scroll with mouse wheel' hint for narrow-terminal usability
features.description: Closes the §7 narrow-terminal layout decision. Empirical measurement (App rendered at 60/70/80/90/100/120 cols) showed the StatusBar is the only region that breaks under narrow width — Header (3× width:1fr buttons), ChatLog (soft-wrap markdown, overflow-x hidden), and Composer (TextArea soft-wrap) all scale gracefully. The StatusBar is fixed-format non-wrapping text; its length grew to 119 chars once the chat log overflowed because of the appended ' | scroll with mouse wheel' hint (added in f-tui-mouse-wheel-scroll). Dropping the hint reduces max StatusBar width from 119 → 93 cols (85 at session start). Mouse-wheel scrolling is the default intuitive behavior, so the hint mostly added width. features.name: TUI Header: per-section toggle + collapse mechanisms (UX fix)
features.description: Refactor of f-tui-header-summary-rail (commit 61cda27) to fix two UX issues user reported 2026-06-20: (a) MCP/todo/subagent sections shared one expand key — clicking once expanded all three. Now each section is an independently clickable HeaderSectionButton; clicking expands only that section; clicking another section switches; clicking same collapses. Only ONE overlay visible at a time (mutual exclusion). (b) After expansion there was no collapse mechanism. Now: ESC key collapses (per spec §4.3.2), clicking the same section button again collapses, clicking anywhere outside the overlay (chat log/status bar/composer) collapses. Click on overlay CONTENT itself does NOT collapse (user is reading). Implementation: Header refactored from Static to Horizontal with 3 HeaderSectionButton children (mcp/todo/subagent); HeaderOverlay now takes (section, state) constructor args and renders only that section (was rendering all 3 sections previously); App gets new BINDING escape → action_collapse_header, new on_click handler that consumes Header/HeaderOverlay clicks and collapses on any other click, new on_header_section_toggle handler with expand/switch/collapse logic; per-section overlay IDs (header-overlay-mcp/todo/subagent) avoid DuplicateIds on switch (old overlay may still be in DOM pending async removal). features.name: TUI test reliability: wait_for_state helper + App.on_event ScreenStackError defense
features.description: Fix pre-existing test flake in wheel-scroll tests (test_status_bar.py × 4 + test_tui_manual_scroll.py × 3) where `assert chat_log.scroll_y < baseline` raced with a fixed `await pilot.pause(0.1)`. Replace the fixed pause with a poll-until-true helper. Also fix a related production robustness issue surfaced by the change: `AgentTUIApp.on_event` did not defend against `ScreenStackError` when `super().on_event(event)` called `self.screen._forward_event(event)` after the screen stack had been torn down (race during message-pump teardown). The flake manifested as `assert 115 < 115` under system load.
features.verification: ./init.sh && uv run python -m loom.cli eval --fail-under 100 (3 consecutive runs)
features.evidence: uv run pytest -q → 453 passed (3 consecutive runs: 85-91s each, no flakes); uv run python -m loom.cli eval --fail-under 100 → 224/224 passed (3 consecutive runs, ~5min each, no flakes); uv run python -m loom.cli audit . → 100/100, all 6 dimensions 5/5; ./init.sh → 'Verification Complete (all green)'; uv run ruff check . → clean; uv run mypy loom/ → clean. Implementation: (1) tests/conftest.py — added `async def wait_for_state(pilot, predicate, *, timeout=2.0, interval=0.02, message='') -> None` that polls until the predicate is truthy or raises AssertionError with the final predicate value; (2) tests/test_status_bar.py — replaced 4 fixed `pilot.pause(0.1)` / `pilot.pause(0.2)` calls after wheel events with `wait_for_state(predicate=lambda: chat_log.scroll_y < baseline, timeout=2.0)`; (3) tests/test_tui_manual_scroll.py — same for 3 tests (up, down, bubble-from-markdown); (4) loom/tui/app.py — added defensive `try/except ScreenStackError: return` around `super().on_event(event)` and `from textual.app import ScreenStackError`. Note: the project pin textual>=0.85.0 resolved to 8.2.7 (a custom/forked build) which has no `Pilot.wait_for` API; `wait_for_state` is a self-contained substitute that mirrors the same predicate-based wait semantic. NOT a behavior change in any user-facing code path — the wheel test assertions are still 'chat_log scrolled by the wheel event' but now wait for the actual outcome instead of a guessed delay. The App.on_event defense is a one-line production change that swallows the screen-stale race during teardown; the event is dropped (no action) because there is no screen to act on it.

features.name: Fix TUI extra '◦ thought · 0s' marker on every turn
features.description: User reported (2026-06-21): every conversation turn shows TWO '◦ thought' markers — the first at '0s' (extra) and the second at the real elapsed time. Root cause: per f-tui-thinking-per-llm-call, the TUI wires BOTH on_message_start and on_assistant_message_start callbacks to AssistantTurnStart, so the App's on_assistant_turn_start handler runs twice within the same turn. Each call to show_thinking_spinner dismissed the just-mounted thinking widget and mounted a fresh one, producing the extra '0s' marker. Fix: add idempotency check in ChatLog.show_thinking_spinner — if _thinking_widget is already mounted and not yet complete, early-return (reuse existing widget). Plus regression-guard eval case (tui-thinking-spinner-no-double-mount-within-turn) that asserts 2 AssistantTurnStart posts in same turn mount only 1 thinking widget.

features.name: Tool display 与 ThinkingMarker 对齐 (3 bug)
features.description: Three bugs violated the design intent ('click to expand inline, same as thinking content'). (1) Inline expand rendered blank: bare tool output fed to Markdown collapsed single newlines into spaces (Markdown soft-break rule), producing one overlong paragraph that overflowed horizontally and looked empty. (2) Double-click opened a separate ToolCallModal — wrong because ThinkingMarker has no modal path. (3) After agent turn ended, the expanded panel of a completed tool went blank — Markdown's async update + display-toggle interaction left content un-rendered after the layout recalculation. Final fix: replace Markdown with Static (synchronous update, no async parse/layout race; markup=False so newlines preserved verbatim); remove double-click-modal path entirely (delete ToolCallMarker.on_click chain==2 branch + _open_modal method + entire ToolCallModal class + ModalScreen/Button imports); on_click unconditionally calls _toggle_output, matching ThinkingMarker. _as_code_block/_fence_for helpers removed (Static doesn't need code-fence wrapping for newlines). CollapsibleToolOutput CSS adds explicit `height: auto` so the widget sizes to content instead of falling back to Vertical's `height: 1fr`. Dead-code cleanup mirrors f-tool-display-p0 which removed the orphan ToolCallCard.

features.name: Phase 1 P0: Agent-quality eval framework (the compass)
features.description: THE blocker for the post-skeleton roadmap. Cases live in loom/eval/cases/agent_quality/. New `loom eval --kind agent-quality` selector separates from harness evals. Baseline pass rate is the calibration point for all subsequent Phase 1 fixes (grep, edit-file v2, max-turns guard) — each must measurably improve the agent-quality score.

Each case spawns `loom run` as a subprocess against an isolated git-initialized tmp workdir under /tmp/loop-eval/agent-quality/<case-name>/, captures the resulting git diff, and runs deterministic judge asserts on file state + executable behavior. features.name: Phase 1 P0: Native grep tool (structured search)
features.description: Most-needed missing tool. Add a structured-output grep tool to the agent's native toolset so the agent can search the codebase for patterns. Implementation: prefer `rg` (ripgrep) if on PATH, fall back to Python `re` + `Path.rglob`. Signature: grep(pattern, path='.', glob=None, case_insensitive=False). Output: each match formatted as `path:line:content` (content truncated to 200 chars), capped at 200 matches with explicit `[...N more matches]` footer. Workspace-bound. is_read_only=True, is_concurrent_safe=True. Must also expose to SUB_TOOLS so subagents can search. Tool description must teach the agent to use grep BEFORE reading files when looking for symbols. features.description: run_lsp_rename_symbol 拿 LSP WorkspaceEdit -> 解析 -> 通过真 PermissionRule 二次校验受影响文件 -> apply。Permission 不绕过(R3 CRITICAL):permissions.py 加 _lsp_rename_outside_workspace + DEFAULT_POLICY.rules + matches_rule public 方法。Rollback 防 SIGKILL(R2):写 /tmp/loom-lsp-rollback-$PID.json journal,SessionStart 扫描死 PID 孤儿 journal 警告。Unix-only URI(R8)显式注释。绝不构造 fake_block(Oracle CRITICAL)。See .sisyphus/plans/loop-lsp-p3.md.
features.verification: uv run pytest tests/test_lsp_rename.py tests/test_lsp_permission.py -v && uv run python -m loom.cli eval --filter lsp-rename --fail-under 100
features.evidence: 8 changes + 4 Oracle amendments, all in scope (5 modified + 4 new), no out-of-scope edits. R3 CRITICAL fix verified: grep -rn fake_block loom/agent/ returns ZERO matches. (1) loom/agent/permissions.py (+36): _lsp_rename_outside_workspace(args) per Oracle Amendment A (workdir = Path.cwd().resolve() moved above the if not files branch for consistency). Early pass (no _resolved_files) falls back to _outside_workspace({'path': args.get('path')}). Late pass iterates _resolved_files, each Path(f).resolve().relative_to(workdir) — ValueError/OSError → True (block). New PermissionRule(tools=('lsp_rename_symbol',), check=_lsp_rename_outside_workspace, message='LSP rename would change files outside the workspace') added to DEFAULT_POLICY.rules. NO matches_rule method added (Oracle Amendment B — find_rule already exists and is sufficient). (2) loom/agent/lsp_apply.py (NEW, ~210 lines): parse_workspace_edit(edit) -> dict[Path, list[dict]] with urlparse+unquote, sorts edits reverse by (start.line, start.character), Unix-only docstring (R8), non-file:// scheme → ValueError, documentChanges without changes → NotImplementedError, INCLUDES entries with empty edits lists (Oracle watch-out). apply_text_edits(text, edits) -> str, 0-indexed character splice. _write_journal(originals) writes /tmp/loom-lsp-rollback-<PID>.json with {pid, files: {abs_path: sha256}} (R2, sha256-only to prevent unbounded temp files). _delete_journal() unlinks missing_ok=True. apply_workspace_edit(edit) -> dict[Path, str]: parse → read all originals + compute new_contents (any fail → raise, no disk write) → _write_journal BEFORE any os.replace → for each file write .tmp + os.replace, record in `written` list → success: _delete_journal + return new_contents → exception: in-process rollback (restore from originals for each `written` path); if rollback itself fails, log error AND keep journal on disk for SessionStart recovery. Docstring includes Oracle Amendment D warning: 'Caller is responsible for verifying all resolved paths in edit are within the workspace before calling this function.' recover_stale_journals(workdir) -> list[Path]: glob /tmp/loom-lsp-rollback-*.json, skip alive-PID journals (_pid_alive via os.kill(pid, 0)), log warning listing stale ones, do NOT auto-restore. (3) loom/agent/tools.py (+52): run_lsp_rename_symbol rewritten with 2-pass permission flow — FIRST PASS: PreToolUse hook fires automatically when LLM calls tool, _lsp_rename_outside_workspace called with LLM-supplied args (no _resolved_files) → falls back to entry-path check via _outside_workspace. LSP call routed through get_or_start + get_server_lock (R5 trace + R7 eviction from PL-2 preserved). SECOND PASS: after LSP returns WorkspaceEdit, parse_workspace_edit → build augmented_args with _resolved_files=[str(p) for p in plan] → DEFAULT_POLICY.find_rule('lsp_rename_symbol', augmented_args) → if non-None return f'Rename blocked by permission policy: {blocking_rule.message}'. Uses DEFAULT_POLICY (not self.policy) intentionally — Oracle Amendment C code comment explains this is non-overridable defense-in-depth against LSP server bugs/malice. apply_workspace_edit does the atomic write + journal. NO fake_block constructed anywhere. (4) loom/agent/loop.py (+14): _on_session_start(event, *args) hook registered for SessionStart, calls lsp_apply.recover_stale_journals(WORKDIR) in try/except (logger.warning on failure, doesn't break other SessionStart callbacks). CRITICAL FIX during verification: initial implementation had signature _on_session_start() (no args) which TypeError'd because trigger_hooks calls callback(event, *args) — every hook callback MUST accept `event` as first positional arg. Fixed to _on_session_start(event, *args) matching _on_session_end(*args) pattern from PL-2. This fix was caught by running full harness eval (3 cases failed: session-start-trigger-no-args, log-hook-session-start-logged, resume-success-rate-benchmark) — subagent had only run the LSP-filtered eval and missed this. (5) tests/test_lsp_permission.py (NEW, 8 tests): test_lsp_rename_rule_in_default_policy, test_lsp_rename_blocks_when_entry_path_outside, test_lsp_rename_blocks_when_any_resolved_file_outside, test_lsp_rename_passes_when_all_inside, test_find_rule_returns_message_string_on_block (NOT matches_rule — skipped per Amendment B), test_find_rule_returns_none_when_passes, test_lsp_rename_handler_calls_find_rule_after_lsp, test_lsp_rename_handler_does_not_construct_fake_block (R3 regression guard via inspect.getsource). features.description: loom 提示词编写质量 vs Claude Code / opencode 对比后改写 4 个最弱段:(1) Agent 角色定义从 3 行中文扩到 7 行,加 OWASP 安全边界 + 完成度锚点(不镀金不半成品/不谎报)+ 读后再改 + 不可逆操作警示 + URL 猜测禁令 + file:line 引用约定,名字从 MiniCode 改为 LoomCode;(2) bash 工具描述从 'Run a shell command.' 1 行扩到 18 行,教 deny list 类别 + 5 条专用工具优先级映射(read_file vs cat/glob vs find/grep vs rg/edit_file vs sed/write_file vs echo)+ 绝对路径 + background + git 安全(NEVER force-push/NEVER commit/--no-verify 禁);(3) task 工具描述从 1 行扩到 40 行,教子 agent 上下文边界(无历史/无 re-delegation/30 turn cap)+ when to use/when NOT to use(5 条专用工具替代路径)+'Brief like a smart colleague' 委派 prompt 写法 + 'Never delegate understanding' 原则 + 4 条 anti-patterns + 3 个 specialized template 指针;(4) SUB_SYSTEM 子 agent 提示词从 5 规则(~150 字符)扩到 15 行 + 4 个 escalation marker + 4 个反模式,教 context assumption(无历史/不知道主 agent 试过什么)+ grep 优先 + read-before-edit + 结构化报告 + 何时 Escalate([UNCLEAR]/[BLOCKED]/[CANNOT_FIX]/[OUT_OF_SCOPE])+ 反模式(no fabricate/no silent skip/no test edit/no destructive)。10 个 eval case 锁住新措辞关键短语防回归。~880 token 增量全部落在 prompt cache 段(static tier + tool schema + SUB_SYSTEM 子 agent 系统提示),首次写入后零边际成本。
